market_context: Markets are riding high on U.S.-Iran peace deal optimism and AMD's blowout earnings that beat on all metrics. Pre-market futures show continued strength with geopolitical tensions easing and AI demand accelerating across semiconductor names.

predictions.reasoning: Strong pre-market momentum (+0.07%) combined with AMD's AI earnings beat creating sector tailwinds. Tech-heavy QQQ should benefit from continued semiconductor rotation and reduced geopolitical risk premium from Iran peace hopes.

AMD's AI surge validates broader chip demand thesis and rising tide lifts all boats. NVDA already gained 5.3% yesterday on AMD's coattails, showing strong correlation. Continued AI infrastructure spending narrative strengthens.

Q1 earnings already reported with mixed results - beat EPS but missed delivery expectations. With semiconductor names stealing spotlight and no new catalysts, TSLA likely to lag broader market strength as attention shifts elsewhere.

Pre-market already showing strength at $414.36. AI infrastructure beneficiary from AMD's validation of CPU demand surge. News about renewable energy delays is minor compared to AI tailwinds driving enterprise cloud adoption.

Oil prices already dropped 3.59% overnight on Iran peace deal hopes. Energy sector was the only red GICS sector yesterday falling 4.2%. Continued diplomatic progress likely to pressure oil prices further, weighing on energy ETF.
